dsl_rule.translation_notes: Translated 'Alexander reversal filter (price move > 2% from recent swing)' to returns > 0.02 for long entries and returns < -0.02 for short entries. Signal is direction-agnostic (context_dependent), so both entry_long and entry_short are specified. Exit conditions are mirror reversals of entry conditions. Target regimes set to TRENDING_UP and TRENDING_DOWN based on signal's focus on trending markets.

backtest_rule.reason: Translated 'Alexander reversal filter (price move > 2% from recent swing)' to returns > 0.02 for long entries and returns < -0.02 for short entries. Signal is direction-agnostic (context_dependent), so both entry_long and entry_short are specified. Exit conditions are mirror reversals of entry conditions. Target regimes set to TRENDING_UP and TRENDING_DOWN based on signal's focus on trending markets.

source_rule_text: Use variable thresholds instead of fixed thresholds when trading trending time series, because fixed thresholds become impractical when the underlying asset exhibits large long-lasting level changes.
